title: Assertion failure when printing external modules with mismatched port locations
body:
**Test commit**
[417a5112947d9889cad54cb5b2110b37978b86b8](https://github.com/llvm/circt/commit/417a5112947d9889cad54cb5b2110b37978b86b8)

**Description**
The IR printer fails an assertion when an `hw.module.extern` has a `port_locs` attribute with a different number of elements than the actual ports defined in the module type.

**Steps to reproduce**
- Minimal MLIR program (test.mlir)
```
#loc = loc("test.mlir":1:1)
"builtin.module"() ({
  "hw.module.extern"() <{
    module_type = !hw.modty<>,
    parameters = [],
    port_locs = [#loc],
    sym_name = "ListModule"
  }> ({}) : () -> ()
}) : () -> ()
```

**Command**
```
circt-opt test.mlir
```

**Output**
```
circt-opt: /workdir/sources/circt/lib/Dialect/HW/HWOps.cpp:1214: SmallVector<mlir::Location> getAllPortLocs(ModTy) [ModTy = circt::hw::HWModuleExternOp]: Assertion `!locs->size() || locs->size() == module.getNumPorts()' failed.
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/circt and include the crash backtrace.
Stack dump:
0.      Program arguments: /workdir/builds/circt/bin/circt-opt test.mlir
 #0 0x000056338509e94b backtrace (/workdir/builds/circt/bin/circt-opt+0x6f4e94b)
```
